attacking_style: Opportunistic finisher who capitalizes on chaos created by teammates' attacks rather than initiating moves himself. Excels in technical summit finishes where positioning trumps raw power.
team_tactics: Team deploys aggressive climbing domestiques (Chaves) to destabilize rivals and create winning opportunities. Benefits from opponents losing their support riders in the finale.
terrain_preference: Summit finishes with technical final kilometers where positioning and bike handling matter. Thrives when rival teams are isolated and cannot control.
vulnerability: Insufficient data to establish clear weakness patterns. Likely vulnerable when opponents maintain full team support into finals.
key_patterns: Wins by perfect wheel selection in chaotic finales, not solo power displays.
Capitalizes when rival teams lose domestique support in closing kilometers.
Excels in technical summit arrivals requiring positioning over sustained climbing power.
form_trajectory: unknown: single race observation insufficient to establish trend, though displayed sharp tactical awareness and finishing speed
